question_text: Explain why the Southern Hemisphere experiences winter at the same time the Northern Hemisphere experiences summer.
correct_answer: Because when one hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun, the other is tilted away from it. When the Northern Hemisphere faces the Sun, the Southern Hemisphere faces away, so they have opposite seasons.
explanation: The Earth's tilted axis means that only one hemisphere can be tilted towards the Sun at any given time. This is why when it is summer in one hemisphere, it is winter in the other.
